title: How to Get a Motorcycle License: Step-by-Step Guide (2026)
description: Learn how to get your motorcycle license step by step in 2026, from permit to road test, plus MSF training courses that can waive your DMV skills test.

children.value: How to Get a Motorcycle License: Step-by-Step Guide

Getting your motorcycle license is more approachable than most new riders expect. While the exact rules are set state by state, the path is broadly the same everywhere: study the rules, pass a knowledge test for a permit, complete a rider training course, and pass (or waive) a skills test. This guide walks through each step and points you to MSF-certified training programs that can make the process faster and safer.
Step 1: Confirm Your State's Requirements

Every state issues motorcycle licenses through its DMV, MVA, or equivalent agency, and the minimum age, permit period, and testing rules vary. Before you start, check the official requirements where you live. Our
motorcycle license requirements by state
guide summarizes the common rules and links to each state program.
Step 2: Get a Motorcycle Learner's Permit

Most states require a learner's permit before you can practice on public roads. You earn it by passing a written knowledge test covering road signs, traffic laws, and safe riding practices. A permit usually carries restrictions, such as no nighttime riding, no passengers, and no highway use. See
motorcycle permit vs license
for how the permit fits into the full process.
Step 3: Take a Motorcycle Safety Course
A formal rider course is the single best investment you can make. Courses built on the
Motorcycle Safety Foundation

(MSF) Basic RiderCourse curriculum combine classroom instruction with hands-on riding, and most provide the training motorcycle and helmet. In many states, completing an approved course also lets you skip the DMV road test entirely.
MSF-Certified Training Providers
1. Motorcycle Safety Foundation

The national organization behind the Basic RiderCourse, MSF sets the curriculum standard that most state programs follow. Its course covers basic controls, safe stopping, shifting, and turning, and provides a recognized path to licensing and insurance discounts.
Learn more about Motorcycle Safety Foundation
2. Motorcycle Safety School
Established in 1999, Motorcycle Safety School offers the MSF Basic RiderCourse, Basic RiderCourse 2, and 3-Wheel courses along with private lessons for new and returning riders.
Learn more about Motorcycle Safety School
3. Motorcycle Training Academy
A full-service provider following the MSF curriculum, offering the Basic RiderCourse for novices plus advanced and three-wheel options.
Learn more about Motorcycle Training Academy
Step 4: Pass the Skills Test (or Earn a Waiver)
The skills test evaluates low-speed control, braking, and cornering. You can either take it at the DMV or, in most states, earn a
DMV test waiver
by completing an approved rider course. The waiver is one of the most popular reasons riders choose a course. Learn how it works in our
MSF Basic RiderCourse & DMV test waiver
guide.
Step 5: Apply for Your Motorcycle Endorsement

Once you pass (or waive) the skills test, you visit your licensing agency to add a motorcycle endorsement to your driver's license or receive a standalone motorcycle license. Bring your permit, course completion card if applicable, identification, and the required fee.
Find Training Near You
Ready to start? Use our
motorcycle classes & license training near me
hub to find an approved program, or jump to a state guide such as
California
Texas
, or
New York
Frequently Asked Questions
How long does it take to get a motorcycle license?

If you already have a car license, many riders complete the process in a few weeks: a day or two to study for and pass the permit test, then a weekend rider course that often includes the skills evaluation. Timelines vary by state and course availability.
Do I need a permit before taking a motorcycle course?
It depends on the state and provider. Some courses welcome complete beginners with no permit, while others require a permit first. Check the specific course requirements when you register.
Can I skip the DMV road test?
In most states, yes. Completing an approved MSF-based course earns a skills test waiver, so you do not have to take the road test at the DMV. See our
test waiver guide
for details.
Do I need a motorcycle license for a scooter or moped?
Sometimes. Lightweight mopeds may only require a regular driver's license, while larger scooters need a motorcycle endorsement. Read
do you need a motorcycle license?
for the breakdown.

Browse all motorcycle training listings
